y_star: Combined sanity 1 Gemma B2 trial converges to residual=0
actions_taken: All 5 module unit sanities GREEN (T1 inventory, T2 ProbeExecutor, T3 parser, T4 loop integration, T5 render)
Combined sanity: 1 Gemma B2 trial on test_generation, max_iter=6 (ignored per v3.8), no_progress=3
Result: iter 7 no_progress halt, 843s wall, residual_trajectory=[2,2,2,2,2,2,2] (stuck from iter 0)
Per-iter passing counts: 14 → 21 → 23 → 36 → 36 → 36 → 36 (progress through iter 3 then frozen)
T4 (v3.7 dominance) rollback fired 3 times at iters 4-6 (tie-break — gemma's passing set frozen at 36)
iter_probes captured count per iter: [0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0] — GEMMA DID NOT USE PROBE TOOL across all 7 iters
Verified inventory + probe tool description WAS in iter 0 prompt (6380 chars, contains '### Probe tool' section, contains ```probe format examples, contains 'not recommendations' disclaimer)
Coverage 78.8% (below target 80%, stuck at iter 3+)
Sample failing tests at iter 6: 7 tests gemma added with edge-case assertions it speculated wrong about — same v3.8 failure pattern minus the probe tool which gemma chose not to invoke
y_t_plus_1: Stop rule (b) fires per spec. Founder rule 3 honored: REFUSED 'gemma incapable' conclusion. v4.0 MECHANISMS ALL VERIFIED WORKING: inventory section rendered correctly in iter 0 prompt; probe parser would have routed any ```probe block correctly; ProbeExecutor would have run any command. GAP: Gemma 4B given the tool but did not engage with it. Probed 0 times across 7 iters. The neutral 'not recommendations' framing (per founder principle no-recommendation) appears to under-cue gemma to use the tool. v4.1 candidate paths (still non-hardcoded, all detection-driven): (a) When pytest verifier's failing tests have specific patterns (AssertionError with expected/got mismatch on numeric/dict shapes), the per-test instruction in pytest verifier can add a Direction-A enhancement: 'before re-asserting, you can probe the function to see what it actually returns on this input'. This is signal-triggered (only when pattern matches), not blanket recommendation. (b) Track historical converged-trial probe usage; in feedback, mention 'converged trials on this scenario used N probes on average' — purely data-driven nudge. (c) Move probe tool description from iter-0 prompt-middle to AFTER per-verifier feedback in each iter — when gemma is reading the failure, the probe option is right there. Founder rule 4 check: v2.x had no probe; v21 had no tool augmentation. v4.0 probe + inventory is genuine new — NOT reinvention. The engagement-nudge in v4.1 would also be new.
verify_command: cat /tmp/v4_0_sanity.log + iter_probes in trial JSON (all zeros) + iter_prompts[0] inspection
verify_output_tail: 0 probes invoked across 7 iters; tool fully available; gemma chose not to engage
causal_diagnosis: v4.0 protocol VERIFIED INTACT: every layer of the stack from inventory scan to probe execution to next-iter prompt prepend works. The combined sanity demonstrated the tool's PRESENCE but not the tool's USE. Gemma 4B with v3.4-v3.8 coaching has demonstrated capacity to follow feedback when it's surfaced as the dominant signal (regression META, cluster META). The probe tool description is currently a SECONDARY signal — explicitly framed as neutral ('not recommendations'). Gemma defaults to its training prior 'write code' rather than 'probe first'. FOUNDER PRINCIPLE SELF-CHECK (mandatory per v4.0 spec): 1) No Trampoline-preset tool calls — VERIFIED. Inventory uses shutil.which, ProbeExecutor runs arbitrary shell. 2) No scenario-specific bias — VERIFIED. Probe description identical across scenarios. 3) Magic constants — only physical resource limits (timeout 60s, stdout 2000, stderr 1000, file cap 200, AST cap 50). No business-logic numbers. 4) No recommendations in probe description — VERIFIED. 'These are not recommendations' explicit. All 4 self-check items pass. The methodology gap is engagement (signal strength), not implementation (tool presence). v4.1 should make probe discoverability adaptive: surface the probe option STRONGER when failure patterns specifically suggest function-execution-would-help (AssertionError numeric/dict mismatches). This is signal-triggered, not blanket. Still non-hardcoded — uses the same verifier-feedback infra v3.6 built.
